title: Company Analysis
description: Students learn to analyse individual companies using annual reports, competitive positioning, management quality and financial health. Builds toward writing a complete investment thesis on a NZ company.
rationale: Deep dive into analysing individual companies. Students learn to read annual reports, assess competitive moats, evaluate management quality and construct a complete investment thesis. Uses real NZ companies throughout.
learningObjectives: Read and extract key information from a NZ company annual report
Identify and assess a company's competitive moat
Evaluate management quality using track record and alignment metrics
Analyse revenue quality, profit margins and earnings sustainability
Write a complete investment thesis for a NZ listed company
assessmentTask: Students produce a complete 2-page investment thesis on one NZ listed company covering: business model, competitive moat, management assessment, key metrics, risks and a buy/hold/sell recommendation with price target rationale.

lessons.title: Competitive Moats — What Makes a Business Durable
Week 1 Challenge — Full Company Deep Dive
Valuation — Estimating What a Company Is Worth
Module 4 Review — Writing Your Investment Thesis
